items.title: Architecture: IDE ↔ DAP ↔ debugpy ↔ your process
items.mermaid:
flowchart LR IDE["IDE UI
(VS Code / PyCharm)"] -- DAP --> Adapter["Debug adapter
(debugpy / pydevd)"] Adapter -- sys.settrace --> Proc["Your Python process"] Proc -- frames / locals --> Adapter Adapter -- events --> IDE
items.caption: The IDE speaks DAP to a small adapter; the adapter is what actually instruments your Python process.

items.title: Three launch modes
items.mermaid:
flowchart TB L["Launch:
IDE starts your program"] A["Attach:
IDE connects to a running process"] T["Test:
IDE runs pytest under debug"] L --> Use1["Most common"] A --> Use2["Long-running services, notebooks, remote pods"] T --> Use3["Stop on test failures; debug fixtures"]
items.caption: Pick the launch mode that matches the workflow — attach is the killer for production-like processes.
